Scenario.title: Ransomware Attack on Healthcare Systems via Unpatched Web Portal
Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple A skilled threat actor exploited a vulnerable healthcare web portal to deploy ransomware, encrypting critical patient data and disrupting hospital operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Internet-facing assets", "Vulnerability", "Custom APIs", "Data and Command Integrity Attack"]

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker exploited a non-production interface (e.g., debug endpoint) in a healthcare web portal, leveraging the unpatched vulnerability CVE-2010-1297 to gain unauthorized access to the system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker executed PowerShell scripts through compromised API endpoints to establish control over the system, bypassing basic firewall protections and escalating privileges within the network.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Ransomware was deployed to encrypt patient records and critical infrastructure data, rendering systems inaccessible and demanding payment for decryption keys, causing operational paralysis in healthcare facilities.

Scenario.summary: A skilled threat actor exploited a vulnerable healthcare web portal using a known vulnerability (CVE-2010-1297), executed malicious scripts to gain deeper access, and deployed ransomware to encrypt critical data, severely disrupting hospital operations.
